purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
inputs: Canonical .sdlc stories, requirements, contracts, traces, tests, approvals, output links, and releases.
outputs: Additive view-model lineage dossiers plus a responsive Asked/Decided/Contract/Done/Verified presentation.
Story-scoped implementation evidence and automated backend, UI, server, package, and browser QA.
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
Each dossier contains only records with explicit linkage to that story and cross-story isolation tests pass.
Recorded rationale_summary reaches the rendered UI separately from generated explanation.
Missing lanes and project-level unlinked evidence are explicit; the app never invents lineage.
Full project checks, installed-plugin smoke tests, desktop/mobile rendered QA, and strict story gate pass.

notes: Build dossiers only from explicit canonical linkage.
Run browser QA only against the loopback observatory.

contextualization.summary: Close the remaining Change Observatory acceptance gap by adding a proof-bound dossier per story that preserves explicit links and recorded rationale across request, decision, contract, implementation, verification, and release.

context_sources.excerpt: id REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 kind requirement schema_version requirement v1 title Install-integrated SDLC Change Observatory summary Provide a local visual application shipped inside the Agentic SDLC plugin that reconstructs what was requested decided changed verified and released across stories phases contracts and iterations for technical and non-technical readers. status proposed acceptance_criteria After plugin installation agentic-sdlc observe --root <project> launches the included application without a separate build or cloud service. The overview answers What was asked? What changed? and Why was it decided? from canonical .sdlc evidence and links every claim to source records. The timeline shows story iterations across Discovery Analysis Design Implementation Validation and Release distinguishing recorded inferred missing blocked and complete states. Contracts approvals decisions assumptions risks changed files tests gates handoffs sync events and release evidence are navigable with raw-record access. Natural-language explanations are clearly labeled as generated contain no private chain-of-thought and expose only stored rationale inputs outputs alternatives and evidence. T

# Change Observatory Change Observatory is the visual local-first lineage reader bundled with Agentic SDLC. It turns canonical `.sdlc/` records into a presentation that a non-technical stakeholder can start with and a technical reviewer can drill through to raw evidence. ## Launch After Installation In Codex ask ```text Open the Change Observatory for this project. ``` The installed `Change Observatory` skill resolves its own plugin root and runs the plugin-local CLI. It does not assume that `agentic-sdlc` is globally available in `PATH`. An npm Git or tarball installation that exposes the package bin can launch it directly ```bash agentic-sdlc observe --root /path/to/project ``` Automation can suppress browser opening and consume the first NDJSON event ```bash agentic-sdlc observe \ --root /path/to/project \ --host 127.0.0.1 \ --port 0 \ --no-open \ --json ``` `observatory.ready.url` is the browser URL. Keep the process alive while using the application and stop it with `SIGINT` or `SIGTERM`. ## What It Shows - the recorded request and requirement behind an iteration; - what changed grouped by recorded intent where available; - decisions approvals rationale summaries alternatives

questions.question: What linkage may the dossier use?
questions.answer: Only explicit story_id, requirement_id, related IDs, contract IDs, and evidence paths from canonical records.

questions.question: May the browser generate missing history?
questions.answer: No. Missing, malformed, and unlinked states must remain visible and no LLM runs in the app.

constraints: Keep the application local, read-only, dependency-free, build-free, and backward compatible with change-observatory:view:v1.
Never associate records by timestamp proximity, filename similarity, or free-text semantics.
Preserve rationale_summary separately from generated explanation and never expose private reasoning.

approvals.summary: Approved under Antonio's delegated lineage-completion scope: one local-only additive dossier implementation using explicit canonical links, separate recorded rationale, fail-closed missing states, no new dependency or external boundary, and full backend/UI/installed-plugin verification.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Correct only the new Observatory lineage capability and contract records so their local-only work brief contains no install, external, deployment, secret, production, or destructive approval boundary; then approve the corrected exact records.
